name: Hunyuan (1st-gen)
note: Tencent's first in-house foundation model (100B+ params, 2T+ pretraining tokens), launched for enterprises via Tencent Cloud API and wired into 50+ Tencent products.
verificationNotes:
Confirmed existence and release (Sept 7, 2023, Global Digital Ecosystem Summit) plus '100B+ params, 2T+ tokens' claim via Tencent's own announcement article and corroborating press (Yahoo Finance/SCMP/Pandaily). FLAGGED, NOT CORRECTED: ctxK=4 (4K context) could not be independently confirmed — the cited source article does not mention context length at all, and no other primary source could be found tying a specific context-window figure to this original Sept-2023 release (later 2024-era Hunyuan API tiers like hunyuan-lite show 3K-28K depending on interface, unrelated to this model). Schema requires a numeric ctxK so the original value was kept, but treat it as low-confidence/unverified rather than confirmed. paramsB kept null since only '100B+' was ever disclosed, no exact figure found. No results/benchmark rows were present to verify. ROUND-7 EXHAUSTIVE-SEARCH RECORD (2026-07-15, methodology-diversified per native-language/regional-leaderboard instructions): No qualifying benchmark result found for Hunyuan (1st-gen, Sept 2023, Tencent) after a widened, Chinese-language-first search. Checked: Tencent's own Chinese press coverage (news.qq.com, 36Kr, QbitAI/量子位, Leiphone/雷峰网, Zhihu, Baidu Baike [blocked]), the official Tencent Cloud product-brief PDF (main.qcloudimg.com/raw/document/product/pdf/1729_97729_cn.pdf — fully read, no historical benchmark table, describes only the current 2025/2026 product line), C-Eval's official leaderboard (Hunyuan/Tencent absent entirely — confirmed via llm-stats.com mirror since cevalbenchmark.com has an expired TLS cert), SuperCLUE/cluebenchmarks.com (no 2023-era Hunyuan entry; Hunyuan scores only start appearing from Aug 2024 onward and are attributed to later model variants like Hunyuan-Turbo-Preview, not this 1st-gen model), OpenCompass/司南, Artificial Analysis (tracks only Hunyuan Video and 2026-era Hy3/Hy3-preview, never the 2023 closed-API 1st-gen model), and OpenRouter (only lists Hunyuan-A13B and Hy3, both post-2024 open-weight releases; the 1st-gen closed China-only API was never distributed there). Wayback Machine (web.archive.org) could not be fetched in this environment. Every source repeats the same non-numeric marketing claim from Tencent's own Sept 2023 launch article ("excellent performance on MMLU/C-Eval/AGI-eval") with zero actual scores ever published, and the model does not appear on any scored public leaderboard from that period. This looks like a genuine case where the vendor never released a citable numeric benchmark result for this specific model — recommend concluding no public data exists rather than continuing to search. ROUND-13 STRUCTURED-SOURCE CHECK (2026-07-15, Epoch AI raw benchmark feed + Vals.ai leaderboards, checked directly): No new benchmark results found for Hunyuan (1st-gen) (Tencent, Sept 2023) after checking the two specified structured sources plus two backups. Result: zero citable numeric scores exist anywhere checked.

1. Epoch AI raw data feed — fetched both https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.json (179KB, benchmark-definitions/metadata only, 20 benchmark keys) and https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.csv (2.29MB, the actual model x benchmark results table, header row confirms columns like model, Best score, Organization, Hugging Face developer id, etc.). Case-insensitive grep for "hunyuan" across benchmarks.json found exactly one hit, and it was unrelated to model results: a description of the CL-bench benchmark noting it was "created by the Tencent Hunyuan team" (i.e. Hunyuan researchers built a benchmark, not a Hunyuan model being scored on one). [ROUND 20 — HF-raw-content + academic-citation search, 2026-07-16] No qualifying benchmark result found for Hunyuan (1st-gen) (Tencent, Sept 2023) via either of the two specified source types. Both were checked thoroughly; details below.

(1) HuggingFace model card check: This model has NO HuggingFace repo. Directly checked huggingface.co/tencent (140 models listed) and huggingface.co/Tencent-Hunyuan (25 models listed), plus a creation-date-sorted search for 'tencent hunyuan' across all of HF. Every repo under both orgs is a later-generation, separately-branded release: HunyuanDiT (text-to-image, mid-2024), HunyuanVideo/HunyuanVideo-1.5 (2024-2025), Hunyuan3D-2/2.1 (2024-2025), HunyuanImage-3.0 (2025), Tencent-Hunyuan-Large / Hunyuan-A13B-Instruct / Hunyuan-1.8B-Instruct / Hunyuan-7B-Pretrain (2024-2025 open-weight LLMs), Hy3, HunyuanOCR, Hunyuan-MT-7B (2025-2026). None is the original Sept-2023 100B+-param closed-API foundation model this corpus entry describes -- that model was never open-weighted or mirrored to HF (consistent with it being API-only via Tencent Cloud), so there is no model card, no 'Evaluation results' metadata table, and no eval_results.json to check. Confirmed via two independent WebFetch reads of the org pages plus a dedicated HF search sorted by creation date.

(2) Academic citation search: This model also has NO original arXiv/academic technical report -- unlike ERNIE 3.0 Titan (arXiv 2112.12731), Tencent announced the 1st-gen Hunyuan solely via its own Sept 7 2023 press release/blog (tencent.com/en-us/articles/2201685.html) at the Global Digital Ecosystem Summit, not a paper. Later Hunyuan generations DO have arXiv technical reports -- Hunyuan-Large (2411.02265, Nov 2024), Hunyuan-TurboS (2505.15431), Hunyuan-MT (2509.05209), HunyuanImage 3.0 (2509.23951), HunyuanVideo 1.5 (2511.18870), HunyuanOCR (2511.19575) -- and I checked all of these plus broader searches ("compared with Hunyuan", "including Hunyuan" + benchmark table, SecBench 2412.20787) for any citation of the specific 1st-gen 2023 model; all references found are to these later models (e.g. SecBench cites 'Tencent Hunyuan-Turbo', a 2024+ model), never the 1st-gen. The Semantic Scholar API (api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1/paper/search) returned HTTP 429 'Too Many Requests' on every one of 5 separate attempts over several minutes in this sandbox (shared-IP rate limit, not resolvable by retrying), so I substituted equivalent WebSearch queries (site-restricted and citation-phrasing searches, Google Scholar result surfacing) covering the same ground; no citing paper reporting a 1st-gen Hunyuan benchmark score in a comparison table was found. One tangential non-benchmark hit: a Bulletin of CAS commentary piece (bulletinofcas.researchcommons.org/journal/vol39/iss9/16) mentions Tencent's large-model progress only in vague prose ('achieved exceptional performance in multi-modal applications'), no numeric scores.

Conclusion: this remains a genuine no-public-benchmark-data case for the 1st-gen model specifically -- it predates Tencent's practice (adopted only from later Hunyuan generations onward) of publishing arXiv technical reports and HF-hosted model cards with evaluation tables, and it was never open-weighted for community benchmarking. Recommend concluding no citable result exists after this round rather than continuing to search the same two source types.

[ROUND 21 — light delta-check, 2026-07-16] Re-checked for any benchmark data that could plausibly have surfaced since Round 20 (also dated 2026-07-16, same day). A targeted web search for "Tencent Hunyuan 2023 first generation benchmark score MMLU C-Eval" surfaced only the same later-generation Hunyuan releases already ruled out in prior rounds (Hunyuan-Large, Hunyuan T1, Hunyuan-TurboS, Hunyuan-MT) — no result ties any numeric score to the Sept-2023 1st-gen closed-API model. No plausible new source (no fresh HF repo, arXiv report, or leaderboard listing) has appeared in the hours since Round 20. Conclusion unchanged: zero citable benchmark results exist for this model; all 9 core-benchmark slugs remain correctly omitted for lack of evidence.
